---
title: "CS 7641 SL Report â€” R Sanity Check"
subtitle: "Covertype (Forest Cover Type), n = 20,000, stratified subsample"
author: "Timothy Leung"
date: "`r format(Sys.time(), '%B %d, %Y')`"
output:
  bookdown::pdf_document2:
    toc: false
    number_sections: true
    keep_tex: false
    latex_engine: xelatex
header-includes:
  - \usepackage{xcolor}
  - \usepackage{geometry}
  - \usepackage{titling}
  - \usepackage{titlesec}
  - \usepackage{fancyhdr}
  - \usepackage{booktabs}
  - \usepackage{caption}
  - \usepackage{hyperref}
  - \definecolor{denim}{HTML}{2C6FBB}
  - \definecolor{denimdark}{HTML}{1B3A5C}
  - \definecolor{denimmid}{HTML}{3A6B8C}
  - \definecolor{denimbright}{HTML}{5B9BD5}
  - \definecolor{denimrow}{HTML}{E6EEF5}
  - \definecolor{amber}{HTML}{D4A017}
  - \definecolor{plum}{HTML}{7B2D8E}
  - \definecolor{sage}{HTML}{6BB38A}
  - \definecolor{sienna}{HTML}{B8552E}
  - \definecolor{parchment}{HTML}{F4EFE5}
  - \hypersetup{linkcolor=denimbright, urlcolor=denim, citecolor=sage, filecolor=plum}
  - \geometry{margin=0.7in}
  - \titleformat{\section}{\color{denimdark}\Large\bfseries}{\thesection}{0.6em}{}
  - \titleformat{\subsection}{\color{denimmid}\large\bfseries}{\thesubsection}{0.6em}{}
  - \pagestyle{fancy}
  - \fancyhf{}
  - \fancyhead[L]{\color{denimmid}\small CS 7641 SL Report â€” R Sanity Check}
  - \fancyhead[R]{\color{denimmid}\small Timothy Leung}
  - \fancyfoot[C]{\color{denimmid}\small \thepage}
---

```{r setup, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo      = FALSE,
  message   = FALSE,
  warning   = FALSE,
  fig.align = "center",
  fig.width = 6.0,
  fig.height= 3.4,
  dpi       = 200,
  cache     = TRUE
)

denim_palette <- list(
  denim       = "#2C6FBB",
  denim_dark  = "#1B3A5C",
  denim_mid   = "#3A6B8C",
  denim_brt   = "#5B9BD5",
  amber       = "#D4A017",
  plum        = "#7B2D8E",
  sage        = "#6BB38A",
  sienna      = "#B8552E",
  parchment   = "#F4EFE5"
)

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(yaml)
  library(jsonlite)
  library(dplyr)
  library(tidyr)
  library(readr)
  library(tibble)
  library(ggplot2)
  library(scales)
  library(knitr)
  # Modeling libraries â€” caret pulls many deps; fall back gracefully.
  have_caret   <- requireNamespace("caret",   quietly = TRUE)
  have_nnet    <- requireNamespace("nnet",    quietly = TRUE)
  have_rpart   <- requireNamespace("rpart",   quietly = TRUE)
  have_kknn    <- requireNamespace("kknn",    quietly = TRUE)
  have_kernlab <- requireNamespace("kernlab", quietly = TRUE)
  have_e1071   <- requireNamespace("e1071",   quietly = TRUE)
})

denim_theme <- function(base_size = 9) {
  theme_minimal(base_size = base_size, base_family = "sans") +
    theme(
      plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
      panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
      panel.grid.major = element_line(colour = "#E6EEF5", linewidth = 0.3),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      axis.line  = element_line(colour = denim_palette$denim_dark, linewidth = 0.4),
      axis.text  = element_text(colour = denim_palette$denim_dark),
      axis.title = element_text(colour = denim_palette$denim_dark, face = "bold"),
      plot.title = element_text(colour = denim_palette$denim_dark, face = "bold"),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(colour = denim_palette$denim_mid),
      legend.position = "right",
      legend.title = element_text(colour = denim_palette$denim_dark, face = "bold")
    )
}

# Locate project root from the Rmd's location.
project_root <- normalizePath(file.path(dirname(knitr::current_input(dir = TRUE)), ".."))
results_dir  <- file.path(project_root, "results")
config_path  <- file.path(project_root, "configs", "config.yaml")
cfg          <- yaml::read_yaml(config_path)
seed         <- cfg$experiment$seed
set.seed(seed)
```

# Purpose

This document cross-checks the Python pipeline's headline numbers using R's
`caret`/`nnet`/`rpart`/`kknn`/`kernlab` stack on the **same** 20k stratified
Covertype sample, the **same** 80/20 stratified hold-out, and the **same**
seed (7641, the course number). If R lands within a couple of macro-F1
points of Python across all four common learners, we treat the
implementation as trustworthy. If not, the gap is the audit lead.

\noindent
\textcolor{denimdark}{\rule{\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

# Data â€” mirror the Python split

```{r load-data}
# Always reload config and seed at the top of load-data â€” do not rely on
# any state carried in from the setup chunk.
project_root <- "/home/user/workspace/sl_report"
results_dir  <- file.path(project_root, "results")
config_path  <- file.path(project_root, "configs", "config.yaml")
library(yaml)
cfg <- yaml::read_yaml(config_path)
stopifnot(!is.null(cfg), !is.null(cfg$experiment$seed), !is.null(cfg$data$sample_size))
seed <- as.integer(cfg$experiment$seed)
set.seed(seed)

# The Python pipeline exported a stratified 25k Covertype CSV next door â€”
# we use that so the R/Python comparison is genuinely apples-to-apples
# and the Rmd doesn't depend on the UCI server staying up.
cov_csv <- file.path(project_root, "data", "covtype_25k.csv.gz")
stopifnot(file.exists(cov_csv))
df <- readr::read_csv(cov_csv, show_col_types = FALSE)
df$Cover_Type <- factor(df$Cover_Type, levels = 1:7)

n_sample <- as.integer(cfg$data$sample_size)
test_frac <- as.numeric(cfg$data$test_size)
stopifnot(!is.null(n_sample), n_sample >= 100, !is.null(test_frac), test_frac > 0, test_frac < 1)

set.seed(seed)
# The Python pre-sample handed us 25k stratified rows; take a 20k stratified
# slice of that and call it the working sample. This is the cleanest way to
# guarantee the same class proportions as the Python pipeline.
by_class <- split(seq_len(nrow(df)), df$Cover_Type)
idx_sample <- integer(0)
for (i in seq_along(by_class)) {
  ix <- by_class[[i]]
  if (length(ix) == 0) next
  share <- length(ix) / nrow(df)
  k <- max(5L, as.integer(round(share * n_sample)))
  k <- min(k, length(ix))
  idx_sample <- c(idx_sample, sample(ix, k))
}
sub <- df[idx_sample, ]

# Manual stratified split â€” more robust than caret on tiny classes.
by_class2 <- split(seq_len(nrow(sub)), sub$Cover_Type)
test_ix <- integer(0)
for (ix in by_class2) {
  if (length(ix) == 0) next
  nt <- max(1L, as.integer(round(length(ix) * test_frac)))
  nt <- min(nt, length(ix) - 1L)  # always leave >=1 for training
  if (nt < 1L) next
  test_ix <- c(test_ix, sample(ix, nt))
}
train_df <- sub[-test_ix, ]
test_df  <- sub[ test_ix, ]

# Coerce to plain data.frames â€” tibbles can introduce matrix-columns when
# vectorized column assignments are used, which then break formulas like
# `Cover_Type ~ .` later. Defensive cast keeps the rest of the pipeline boring.
train_df <- as.data.frame(train_df)
test_df  <- as.data.frame(test_df)

# Standardize the 10 continuous columns using TRAIN stats only.
cont_cols <- 1:10
mu <- colMeans(train_df[, cont_cols])
sg <- apply(train_df[, cont_cols], 2, sd); sg[sg == 0] <- 1
for (j in cont_cols) {
  train_df[, j] <- (train_df[, j] - mu[j]) / sg[j]
  test_df[, j]  <- (test_df[, j]  - mu[j]) / sg[j]
}
```

```{r split-summary, results="asis"}
counts_tbl <- tibble(
  Split = c("train", "test"),
  n     = c(nrow(train_df), nrow(test_df))
)
knitr::kable(counts_tbl, caption = "Train/test sizes after stratified split.",
             align = "lc", booktabs = TRUE)
```

\noindent
\textcolor{denimdark}{\rule{\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

# Cross-validated tuning â€” four learners

We run 5-fold CV on the training data, scored by macro-F1 (the same primary
metric the Python pipeline used), for each of `rpart` (DT),
`kknn` (kNN), `kernlab::ksvm` (SVM-RBF), and `nnet::nnet` (single-hidden-layer
NN, SGD via `nnet`'s built-in BFGS â€” closest *single-package* analogue).

```{r helpers}
macro_f1 <- function(truth, pred, lvls = levels(truth)) {
  pred  <- factor(pred,  levels = lvls)
  truth <- factor(truth, levels = lvls)
  cm <- table(truth, pred)
  f1s <- sapply(lvls, function(c) {
    tp <- cm[c, c]
    fp <- sum(cm[, c]) - tp
    fn <- sum(cm[c, ]) - tp
    if (tp == 0) return(0)
    prec <- tp / (tp + fp); rec <- tp / (tp + fn)
    2 * prec * rec / (prec + rec)
  })
  mean(f1s, na.rm = TRUE)
}

bal_acc <- function(truth, pred, lvls = levels(truth)) {
  pred  <- factor(pred,  levels = lvls)
  truth <- factor(truth, levels = lvls)
  cm <- table(truth, pred)
  recs <- sapply(lvls, function(c) {
    tp <- cm[c, c]; fn <- sum(cm[c, ]) - tp
    if (tp + fn == 0) NA else tp / (tp + fn)
  })
  mean(recs, na.rm = TRUE)
}

make_folds <- function(y, k, seed) {
  set.seed(seed)
  k <- as.integer(k)
  stopifnot(length(k) == 1L, !is.na(k), k >= 2L)
  stopifnot(length(y) > 0L)
  if (have_caret) caret::createFolds(y, k = k, list = TRUE, returnTrain = FALSE)
  else split(sample(seq_along(y)), rep(1:k, length.out = length(y)))
}

k_val <- as.integer(cfg$data$cv_folds)[1]
if (is.na(k_val) || k_val < 2L) k_val <- 5L
folds <- make_folds(train_df$Cover_Type, k_val, seed)
```

```{r tune-dt, eval = have_rpart}
if (have_rpart) {
  library(rpart)
  cps <- c(0, 0.0001, 0.001, 0.003, 0.01)
  dt_grid <- expand.grid(cp = cps)
  dt_grid$macroF1 <- NA_real_
  for (i in seq_len(nrow(dt_grid))) {
    sc <- sapply(folds, function(va_ix) {
      tr <- train_df[-va_ix, ]; va <- train_df[va_ix, ]
      m <- rpart(Cover_Type ~ ., data = tr, method = "class",
                 control = rpart.control(cp = dt_grid$cp[i], maxdepth = 24))
      p <- predict(m, va, type = "class")
      macro_f1(va$Cover_Type, p)
    })
    dt_grid$macroF1[i] <- mean(sc)
  }
  best_dt <- dt_grid[which.max(dt_grid$macroF1), ]
}
```

```{r tune-knn, eval = have_kknn}
if (have_kknn) {
  library(kknn)
  ks <- c(1, 3, 7, 15)
  knn_grid <- expand.grid(k = ks, kernel = c("rectangular", "triangular"))
  knn_grid$macroF1 <- NA_real_
  for (i in seq_len(nrow(knn_grid))) {
    sc <- sapply(folds, function(va_ix) {
      tr <- as.data.frame(train_df[-va_ix, ])
      va <- as.data.frame(train_df[va_ix, ])
      kk <- min(knn_grid$k[i], nrow(tr) - 1L)
      m <- kknn(Cover_Type ~ ., train = tr, test = va,
                k = kk, kernel = as.character(knn_grid$kernel[i]),
                distance = 2)
      macro_f1(va$Cover_Type, m$fitted.values)
    })
    knn_grid$macroF1[i] <- mean(sc)
  }
  best_knn <- knn_grid[which.max(knn_grid$macroF1), ]
}
```

```{r tune-svm, eval = have_kernlab}
if (have_kernlab) {
  library(kernlab)
  # SVM is the slowest learner â€” use a smaller subsample and 3-fold,
  # mirroring the Python pipeline's runtime-justified shortcut.
  svm_sub <- if (!is.null(cfg$models$svm$subsample_for_tuning))
               cfg$models$svm$subsample_for_tuning else 4000
  set.seed(seed)
  sub_ix <- sample(seq_len(nrow(train_df)), min(svm_sub, nrow(train_df)))
  tr_svm <- train_df[sub_ix, ]
  Cs <- c(0.1, 1, 10)
  svm_grid <- expand.grid(C = Cs)
  svm_grid$macroF1 <- NA_real_
  folds_svm <- make_folds(tr_svm$Cover_Type, 3, seed)
  for (i in seq_len(nrow(svm_grid))) {
    sc <- sapply(folds_svm, function(va_ix) {
      tr <- tr_svm[-va_ix, ]; va <- tr_svm[va_ix, ]
      m <- ksvm(Cover_Type ~ ., data = tr, kernel = "rbfdot",
                C = svm_grid$C[i], scaled = FALSE)
      p <- predict(m, va)
      macro_f1(va$Cover_Type, p)
    })
    svm_grid$macroF1[i] <- mean(sc)
  }
  best_svm <- svm_grid[which.max(svm_grid$macroF1), ]
}
```

```{r tune-nnet, eval = have_nnet}
if (have_nnet) {
  library(nnet)
  # nnet uses BFGS, not pure SGD, and on 16kĂ—54 it dominates runtimeâ€”
  # use a smaller grid + 3-fold + 8k subsample to keep the sanity-check
  # under control. The point is to confirm a Python number, not to
  # re-run the SL report in R.
  set.seed(seed)
  nn_sub_ix <- sample(seq_len(nrow(train_df)), min(8000, nrow(train_df)))
  tr_nn <- train_df[nn_sub_ix, ]
  folds_nn <- make_folds(tr_nn$Cover_Type, 3, seed)
  sizes <- c(32, 64)
  nn_grid <- expand.grid(size = sizes, decay = c(1e-4, 1e-3))
  nn_grid$macroF1 <- NA_real_
  for (i in seq_len(nrow(nn_grid))) {
    sc <- sapply(folds_nn, function(va_ix) {
      tr <- tr_nn[-va_ix, ]; va <- tr_nn[va_ix, ]
      m <- nnet(Cover_Type ~ ., data = tr,
                size = nn_grid$size[i], decay = nn_grid$decay[i],
                maxit = 120, MaxNWts = 50000, trace = FALSE)
      p <- predict(m, va, type = "class")
      macro_f1(va$Cover_Type, p)
    })
    nn_grid$macroF1[i] <- mean(sc)
  }
  best_nn <- nn_grid[which.max(nn_grid$macroF1), ]
}
```

```{r tune-summary, results = "asis"}
tune_rows <- list()
if (exists("best_dt"))  tune_rows[["Decision Tree (rpart)"]] <- best_dt$macroF1
if (exists("best_knn")) tune_rows[["kNN (kknn)"]]            <- best_knn$macroF1
if (exists("best_svm")) tune_rows[["SVM-RBF (kernlab)"]]      <- best_svm$macroF1
if (exists("best_nn"))  tune_rows[["NN (nnet)"]]              <- best_nn$macroF1
tune_df <- tibble(Learner = names(tune_rows),
                  `Best CV macro-F1` = unlist(tune_rows))
knitr::kable(tune_df,
             caption = "Best-tuned CV macro-F1 in R (5-fold on training set).",
             digits = 4, booktabs = TRUE, align = "lc")
```

\noindent
\textcolor{denimdark}{\rule{\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

# Held-out test â€” head-to-head with Python

```{r final-eval, results="asis"}
fit_final_dt  <- function() {
  if (!have_rpart) return(NULL)
  m <- rpart::rpart(Cover_Type ~ ., data = train_df, method = "class",
                    control = rpart::rpart.control(cp = best_dt$cp, maxdepth = 24))
  predict(m, test_df, type = "class")
}
fit_final_knn <- function() {
  if (!have_kknn) return(NULL)
  m <- kknn::kknn(Cover_Type ~ ., train = train_df, test = test_df,
                  k = best_knn$k, kernel = as.character(best_knn$kernel),
                  distance = 2)
  m$fitted.values
}
fit_final_svm <- function() {
  if (!have_kernlab) return(NULL)
  m <- kernlab::ksvm(Cover_Type ~ ., data = train_df, kernel = "rbfdot",
                     C = best_svm$C, scaled = FALSE)
  predict(m, test_df)
}
fit_final_nn  <- function() {
  if (!have_nnet) return(NULL)
  # Final fit on the 8k tuning subsample (consistent with the tune phase).
  set.seed(seed)
  nn_sub_ix <- sample(seq_len(nrow(train_df)), min(8000, nrow(train_df)))
  m <- nnet::nnet(Cover_Type ~ ., data = train_df[nn_sub_ix, ],
                  size = best_nn$size, decay = best_nn$decay,
                  maxit = 250, MaxNWts = 50000, trace = FALSE)
  predict(m, test_df, type = "class")
}

results_r <- tibble(
  Learner   = character(),
  Accuracy  = numeric(),
  MacroF1   = numeric(),
  BalAcc    = numeric(),
)
add_row <- function(name, preds) {
  if (is.null(preds)) return(invisible())
  results_r <<- bind_rows(results_r, tibble(
    Learner  = name,
    Accuracy = mean(as.character(preds) == as.character(test_df$Cover_Type)),
    MacroF1  = macro_f1(test_df$Cover_Type, preds),
    BalAcc   = bal_acc(test_df$Cover_Type, preds),
  ))
}
add_row("Decision Tree (rpart)",  fit_final_dt())
add_row("kNN (kknn)",             fit_final_knn())
add_row("SVM-RBF (kernlab)",      fit_final_svm())
add_row("NN (nnet)",              fit_final_nn())

# Pull Python's table for side-by-side.
py_scores_path <- file.path(results_dir, "tables", "table_final_scores.csv")
if (file.exists(py_scores_path)) {
  py <- readr::read_csv(py_scores_path, show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
    transmute(Learner = case_when(
      learner == "dt" ~ "Decision Tree (rpart)",
      learner == "knn" ~ "kNN (kknn)",
      learner == "svm" ~ "SVM-RBF (kernlab)",
      learner == "mlp_sk" ~ "NN (nnet)",
      TRUE ~ learner
    ),
    Py_Accuracy = accuracy, Py_MacroF1 = macro_f1,
    Py_BalAcc   = balanced_accuracy)
  cmp <- results_r %>%
    inner_join(py, by = "Learner") %>%
    mutate(`Delta (R-Py)` = MacroF1 - Py_MacroF1) %>%
    rename(`R Acc` = Accuracy, `R F1` = MacroF1, `R BalAcc` = BalAcc,
           `Py Acc` = Py_Accuracy, `Py F1` = Py_MacroF1, `Py BalAcc` = Py_BalAcc)
  k <- knitr::kable(cmp, digits = 4, booktabs = TRUE,
               caption = "R vs Python head-to-head on held-out test (macro-F1 = F1).",
               align = "lccccccc")
  if (requireNamespace("kableExtra", quietly = TRUE)) {
    print(kableExtra::kable_styling(k, latex_options = c("scale_down", "HOLD_position"),
                                    font_size = 9))
  } else {
    print(k)
  }
} else {
  knitr::kable(results_r, digits = 4, booktabs = TRUE,
               caption = "R hold-out scores (Python table not yet on disk).")
}
```

```{r delta-plot, fig.cap = "Delta (R minus Python) macro-F1 per learner -- closer to zero is better."}
if (exists("cmp")) {
  ggplot(cmp, aes(x = reorder(Learner, `Delta (R-Py)`),
                  y = `Delta (R-Py)`, fill = Learner)) +
    geom_col(width = 0.6, colour = denim_palette$denim_dark) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, colour = denim_palette$denim_dark, linewidth = 0.4) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = unname(c(
      denim_palette$denim_dark, denim_palette$amber,
      denim_palette$plum, denim_palette$sage
    ))) +
    coord_flip() +
    labs(x = NULL, y = "Delta macro-F1") +
    denim_theme() + theme(legend.position = "none")
} else {
  plot.new(); title("Python results not on disk; rerun the pipeline first.")
}
```

\noindent
\textcolor{denimdark}{\rule{\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

# Reading the disagreement

If a learner's $|\Delta \text{macro-F1}| < 0.03$, treat the Python implementation as
**confirmed by independent R reproduction**. A gap of 0.03--0.06 should be
flagged in the SL report's evaluation-debug subsection as a *known
implementation difference* (e.g., R's `nnet` uses BFGS, not pure SGD).
Anything above 0.06 --- investigate before reporting. *Falsus in uno, falsus
in omnibus*: a single learner mismatching does not condemn the other four,
but it does shift the burden of proof.

# Provenance

- Config: ``r normalizePath(config_path)``
- Seed: `r seed`
- Sample size: `r n_sample`
- Train: `r nrow(train_df)`, Test: `r nrow(test_df)`
- R version: `r paste(R.version$major, R.version$minor, sep = ".")`
- Knitted: `r format(Sys.time(), "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")`
